Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS ELEN MEDER and WALTER MEDER'S Application To Reconsider And To Modify Court Riling.

Plaintiffs' "application to reconsider and to modify court ruling of November 8, 2022, sustaining error" is denied. A reconsideration motion must be "based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law" and a valid reason for not presenting them earlier. (CCP SEC. 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.) The moving party shall state by affidavit "what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." (CCP SEC. 1008.) Plaintiffs fail to comply with CCP 1008.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Plaintiffs' motion to reconsider the Court's June 8, 2021 order is denied. A motion to reconsider must be "based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1008.) "Facts of which the party seeking reconsideration was aware at the time of the original ruling are not new or different." (In re Marriage of Herr (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1463, 1468.) The moving party, via its affidavit, must demonstrate diligence and provide a satisfactory explanation for failing to offer the new or different evidence at the original hearing. (Herr, 174 Cal.App.4th at 1468; see also New York Times Co. v. Superior Court (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 206, 212; Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 689-691.) Here, plaintiffs' motion is unsupported by any showing of "new or different facts, circumstances, or law," and even if it were, plaintiffs do not offer any satisfactory explanation for failing earlier to raise such facts, circumstances, or law. Plaintiffs' request in their motion for reconsideration that the Court compel defendants to disclose documents, expert reports, and other materials is procedurally improper.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
